functions.name: CURL.AWSPresignURL
functions.signature: MBS( "CURL.AWSPresignURL"; AWSAccessKeyId; AWSSecretAccessKey; Region; Service; Path; Domain { ; Verb; Expires; QueryParameters; Headers } )
functions.description: Query presigned URL for Amazon Webservices. This implements AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 pre-signature for credentials. Works also for other compatible S3 services beside Amazon's like the one from Wasabi or Dell ECS.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path for the URL. Should always start with "/".

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: Optional the domain to use. By default we just build it from region and service.

parameters.name: Verb
parameters.description: The HTTP Operation to do. Can be POST, PUT, GET or DELETE. Default is GET.

parameters.name: Expires
parameters.description: Expiration duration. Provides the time period, in seconds, for which the generated presigned URL is valid. For example, 86400 (24 hours). This value is an integer. The minimum value you can set is 1, and the maximum is 604800 (seven days). A presigned URL can be valid for a maximum of seven days because the signing key you use in signature calculation is valid for up to seven days. Default is 86400 for one day.

parameters.name: QueryParameters
parameters.description: List of query parameters to include. Keys and values must be URL encoded. Keys may need to be lowercase.

parameters.name: Headers
parameters.description: Extra HTTP headers to include. Here you can specify various headers to include in signature and request.

examples.label: Try it for a S3 request:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.AWSPresignURL"; 
"AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"; 
"wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY"; 
"us-east-1"; 
"s3"; 
"/test.txt"; 
"examplebucket.s3.amazonaws.com"; 
"GET"; 
86400 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build an URL and use it:
examples.text_code:
# our parameters
Set Variable [
$AWSAccessKeyId
; Value: "AKIAXYQWFBIBSX2I6GEA" ]
Set Variable [
$AWSSecretAccessKey
; Value: "cE5ctB8xXOG3V7F3zoq1W2gg8x52f7r4y7H5ei03" ]
Set Variable [
$Region
; Value: "eu-central-1" ]
Set Variable [
$Bucketname
; Value: "monkeybreadsoftware" ]
Set Variable [
$Filename
; Value: "test.jpg" ]
Set Variable [
$Verb
; Value: "GET" ]
Set Variable [
$Service
; Value: "s3" ]
# Keep domain empty for default, which is service concatted by region
Set Variable [
$Domain
; Value: "" ]
# Build a path
Set Variable [
$Path
; Value: "/" &
$BucketName
& "/" &
$Filename
]
# and get the URL
Set Variable [
$URL
; Value:
MBS
( "CURL.AWSPresignURL";
$AWSAccessKeyId
;
$AWSSecretAccessKey
;
$Region
;
$Service
;
$Path
;
$Domain
;
$Verb
) ]
# try it
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
$URL
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Image
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsJPEG
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> our parameters</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"AKIAXYQWFBIBSX2I6GEA" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$AWSAccessKeyId</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"cE5ctB8xXOG3V7F3zoq1W2gg8x52f7r4y7H5ei03" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$AWSSecretAccessKey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"eu-central-1" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Region</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"monkeybreadsoftware" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Bucketname</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"test.jpg" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"GET" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Verb</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"s3" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Service</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Keep domain empty for default, which is service concatted by region</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Domain</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Build a path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/" & $BucketName & "/" & $Filename </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and get the URL</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.AWSPresignURL"; $AWSAccessKeyId; $AWSSecretAccessKey; $Region; $Service; $Path; $Domain; $Verb ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$URL</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> try it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; $URL) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResultAsJPEG"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="debug "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Cleanup"; $curl) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.AddInputFile
functions.description: Adds input to file data from the container value. Also sets input file length. You need to define input data for file uploads, http posts and http put operations. Using SetInput* functions and AddInput* functions you can construct the input data in several function calls. This is useful if you do a multipart upload with text parts and file content mixed. This is for adding to existing data, but for uploading only a file, you can use CURL.SetInputFile . If you have a file path, please use CURL.OpenInputFile instead.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The container value with the data to upload.

examples.label: Add container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.AddInputFile"; $curl; MyTable::MyContainer )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.AddInputText
functions.description: Adds input data for this transfer to be the text you provide. Also sets input file length. You need to define input data for file uploads, http posts and http put operations. Please make sure you choose the text encoding the other side expects. Using SetInput* functions and AddInput* functions you can construct the input data in several function calls. This is useful if you do a multipart upload with text parts and file content mixed. This is for adding to existing data, but for uploading only a file, you can use CURL.SetInputText .

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding. Default is UTF-8 for version 10.0 or newer and native encoding before. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Add Text:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("CURL.AddInputText";
$curl
; "Hello World"; "utf8")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.AddInputText"; $curl; "Hello World"; "utf8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader
functions.description: Adds to the http header list. While CURL.SetOptionHTTPHeader sets them all, you can call CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader as often as needed to add more HTTP headers. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Values parameter as often as you need.

examples.label: Set a few HTTP headers:
examples.text_code:
Let([
curl =
MBS
("
CURL.New
");
r =
MBS
( "CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader"; curl; "Authorization: Bearer a92eabb9-76f9-42ee-b280-bcd15cb2e9db" );
r =
MBS
( "CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader"; curl; "Content-Type: application/json" );
r =
MBS
( "CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader"; curl; "Expect:"; "Cache-Control: no-cache" );
options =
MBS
("
CURL.Options
"; curl)
]; options)
Example result:
{
"HTTPHeader"
: [
"Authorization: Bearer a92eabb9-76f9-42ee-b280-bcd15cb2e9db"
,
"Content-Type: application/json"
,
"Expect:"
,
"Cache-Control: no-cache"
],
"Verbose"
:
1
,
"NoProgress"
:
0
,
"FollowLocation"
:
1
,
"MaxRedirs"
:
3
,
"NoSignal"
:
1
,
"DisallowUserNameInURL"
:
1
,
"UploadBufferSize"
:
32768
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  curl = MBS("CURL.New");
  r = MBS( "CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader"; curl; "Authorization: Bearer a92eabb9-76f9-42ee-b280-bcd15cb2e9db" );
  r = MBS( "CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader"; curl; "Content-Type: application/json" );
  r = MBS( "CURL.AddOptionHTTPHeader"; curl; "Expect:"; "Cache-Control: no-cache" );
  options = MBS("CURL.Options"; curl)
]; options)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$curl
; "Expect:"; "Cache-Control: no-cache" ) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Text
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
;
CURL Test::TextEncoding
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Debug
Messages ;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]

functions.name: CURL.Cancel
functions.description: Cancels a running transfer in background. Technically set a flag so the background thread will quit soon. Can take a second till transfer ends. Only useful with CURL.PerformInBackground .

examples.label: Cancels a transfer running async or in background:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.Cancel"; $curl )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.CloseDebugFile
functions.description: Closes the debug output file. To create file for debug messages use CURL.CreateDebugOutputFile function. The plugin closes files automatically when you call CURL.Release . Do not call this method while a transfer is running. In case close operation itself fails, the result is a number with the native file system error code.
functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

examples.label: Close debug log file:
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.CloseInputFile
functions.description: Closes the input file. To open file for reading data use CURL.OpenInputFile function. The plugin closes files automatically when you call CURL.Release . Do not call this method while a transfer is running. In case close operation itself fails, the result is a number with the native file system error code.
functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

functions.name: CURL.CreateDebugOutputFile
functions.description: Creates output file for writing debug messages. Debug messages are written to file if you use this functions and debug messages are always collected for calling CURL.GetDebugMessages after the transfer. This can be useful as you can have your FileMaker solution write a copy of debug messages in temporary file. If your client has problems, you can simply read messages from last transfer. If creation of the file fails, you get an error message back, but still your transfer will probably succeed. Do not call this method while a transfer is running. Logging to a file is useful to read messages in case FileMaker crashes while a transfer. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.
functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

examples.label: Write copy of debug messages to a file into temp folder on Mac:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "CURL.CreateDebugOutputFile";
$curl
; "/tmp/LastMessages.txt" )

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.CreateHeaderOutputFile
functions.description: Creates output file for writing header text. Header messages are written to file if you use this functions and header text are always collected for calling CURL.GetHeaders after the transfer. This can be useful as you can have your FileMaker solution write a copy of headers in temporary file. If your client has problems, you can simply read headers from last transfer. If creation of the file fails, you get an error message back, but still your transfer will probably succeed. Do not call this method while a transfer is running. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.
functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

functions.name: CURL.CreateOutputFile
functions.description: Creates output file for downloading data. If you set MBS( " CURL.SetOptionFileOnlyMode "; $curl; 1 ), the data is only written to file and not available with CURL.GetResult* functions. If creation of the file fails, you get an error message back, but still your transfer will probably succeed. Do not call this method while a transfer is running. Sets CURL.SetOptionFileOnlyMode to 1 for version 9.3 or later automatically. Please call CURL.CloseOutputFile later to make sure the file is closed before you process the file. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.
functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

examples.label: Download file via SFTP to file on disk:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
# set download URL, e.g. sftp://monkeybreadsoftware.net/test.jpg
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
SFTP Download::URL
)]
# build file path on disk and open output file
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetValue(
SFTP Download::FileName
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
SFTP Download::FolderPath
;
$name
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.CreateOutputFile";
$curl
;
$path
)]
# login only via password
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
CURL.SetOptionSSHAuthTypes
";
$curl
; 2+8 )]
# set credentials
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionUserName
";
$curl
;
SFTP Download::UserName
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionPassword
";
$curl
;
SFTP Download::Password
)]
# run transfer
Set Field [
SFTP Download::ErrorCode
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
# check debug log for errors
Set Field [
SFTP Download::Debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
# free CURL session
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
)]

examples.label: Batch download a lot of files via FTP/SFTP:
examples.text_code:
# URL field contains ftp or sftp URL with / on end.
# FileList field contains list of files to load
# OutputPath field contains folder path on disk
#
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:1]
Set Variable [
$max
; Value:ValueCount (
SFTP Download::FileList
)]
#
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
#
# login only via password
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
CURL.SetOptionSSHAuthTypes
";
$curl
; 2+8 )]
#
# set credentials
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionUserName
";
$curl
;
SFTP Download::UserName
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionPassword
";
$curl
;
SFTP Download::Password
)]
#
# start loop over file lists
Loop
#
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetValue (
SFTP Download::FileList
;
$i
)]
#
# build file path on disk and open output file
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
SFTP Download::OutputPath
;
$name
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.CreateOutputFile";
$curl
;
$path
)]
#
# set URL with file name
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
SFTP Download::URL
&
$name
)]
#
# run transfer
Set Field [
SFTP Download::ERGEBNIS
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
#
# query debug log
Set Field [
SFTP Download::DEBUG
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
#
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+1]
Exit Loop If [
$max
=
$i
]
#
End Loop
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> URL field contains ftp or sftp URL with / on end.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> FileList field contains list of files to load</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> OutputPath field contains folder path on disk</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( SFTP Download::FileList )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$max</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> login only via password</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.SetOptionSSHAuthTypes"; $curl; 2+8 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set credentials</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUsername"; $curl; SFTP Download::UserName )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionPassword"; $curl; SFTP Download::Password )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> start loop over file lists</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue ( SFTP Download::FileList ; $i )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build file path on disk and open output file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; SFTP Download::OutputPath; $name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.CreateOutputFile"; $curl; $path)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set URL with file name</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; SFTP Download::URL & $name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> run transfer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="SFTP Download" id="99999" name="ERGEBNIS"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query debug log</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="SFTP Download" id="99999" name="DEBUG"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i +1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$max = $i</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.ErrorCode
functions.description: Returns the last CURL error code. Typical error codes are 6 for a wrong domain name in the URL, 67 for wrong name/password combination, 60 for missing SSL settings, 1 for an unsupported protocol. The list of possible error codes:
functions.result: The last error code from CURL.

examples.label: On success puts download in container, else shows failed:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$errorCode
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.ErrorCode";
$curl
)]
Set Variable [
$httpResult
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResponseCode
";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
Download Async::Result
;
$errorCode
]
Set Field [
Download Async::DebugMessages
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
If [
$errorCode
= 0 and
$httpResult
= 200]
Set Field [
Download Async::Progress
; "Done"]
Set Field [
Download Async::Output
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsContainer
";
$curl
)]
Else
Set Field [
Download Async::Progress
; "Failed"]
Set Field [
Download Async::Output
; ""]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.ErrorCode"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorCode</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResponseCode"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$httpResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$errorCode</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="DebugMessages"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$errorCode = 0 and $httpResult = 200</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Done"</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Progress"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResultAsContainer"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Output"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Failed"</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Progress"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Output"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.Features
functions.description: Queries features in the CURL library. Returns JSON with features enabled for the CURL version in use. See also CURL.VersionDetails for more details.

examples.label: Show features:

    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.Features" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.FileInfos
functions.description: Queries file listing as JSON. Requires wild card being enabled ( CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch ) and an URL with a pattern, e.g. * at the end. Only for FTP protocol. The JSON is an array of objects. Each object includes the following values:

examples.label: Query file list with wildcard:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch
";
$curl
; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
CURL Test::URL
& "*" ) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionPassword
";
$curl
;
CURL Test::Password
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionUserName
";
$curl
;
CURL Test::Name
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionVerbose
";
$curl
; 1) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::listing
;
MBS
( "
JSON.Colorize
";
MBS
("CURL.FileInfos";
$curl
)) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionPassword"; $curl; CURL Test::Password) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUsername"; $curl; CURL Test::Name) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: CURL.FormAddKeyContainer
functions.signature: MBS( "CURL.FormAddKeyContainer"; curl; Name; FileName; Container { ; ContentType; ContentHeaders } )
functions.description: Add a section to a multipart/formdata HTTP POST. This adds file form field with a given name and value. Please call CURL.FormFinish after you added all your fields and before you call CURL.Perform .

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the form field.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for this file.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The file content as a container value. Can be PDF, PNG, GIF, BMP, JPEG or other file content.

parameters.name: ContentType
parameters.description: The content type for this file.

examples.label: Add file from container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.FormAddKeyContainer"; $curl; "Attachment"; "paris.jpg"; Test::test)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add SOAP request with MTOM attachment:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.FormAddKeyText
";
$curl
; "request"; "<soap:Envelope><soap:Body><tns:data><xop:include href=\"test.png\"/></tns:data></soap:Body> </soap:Envelope>"; "text/xml") ]

examples.label: Use netverify.com to check a driver license:
examples.text_code:
#Setup transfer
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
; "https://netverify.com/api/netverify/v2/fastfill")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionVerbose
";
$curl
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionUserName
";
$curl
; "25D63FB16966473DA2416454FD468BBD")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionPassword
";
$curl
; "4356789038498914948018924658901622")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionHTTPHeader
";
$curl
; "Accept: application/json"; "Expect:")]
Set Variable [
$metadata
; Value:"{\"type\": \"DRIVING_LICENSE\",\"country\": \"USA\"}"]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.FormAddKeyValue
";
$curl
; "metadata";
$metadata
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.FormAddKeyContainerContentType";
$curl
; "backsideImage"; "test.jpg";
Donor Docs::DocPDF
; "image/jpeg")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.FormFinish
";
$curl
)]
#Run transfer
Set Field [
Donor Docs::ResultCodePost
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
#Check result
Set Field [
Donor Docs::DebugTextPost
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
Donor Docs::ResultTextPost
;
$result
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Setup transfer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; "https://netverify.com/api/netverify/v2/fastfill")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionVerbose"; $curl; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl; "25D63FB16966473DA2416454FD468BBD")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionPassword"; $curl; "4356789038498914948018924658901622")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionHTTPHeader"; $curl; "Accept: application/json"; "Expect:")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{\"type\": \"DRIVING_LICENSE\",\"country\": \"USA\"}"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$metadata</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.FormAddKeyValue";$curl; "metadata"; $metadata)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.FormAddKeyContainerContentType";$curl; "backsideImage"; "test.jpg"; Donor Docs::DocPDF; "image/jpeg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.FormFinish";$curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Run transfer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Donor Docs" id="99999" name="ResultCodePost"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$result</Calculation>
    <Field table="Donor Docs" id="99999" name="ResultTextPost"/>
  </Step>

functions.name: CURL.FormAddKeyFile
functions.signature: MBS( "CURL.FormAddKeyFile"; curl; Name; Path { ; ContentType; ContentHeaders } )
functions.description: Add a section to a multipart/formdata HTTP POST. This adds a file with the given key name into the form. Please call CURL.FormFinish after you added all your fields and before you call CURL.Perform . This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the form field.

parameters.name: ContentType
parameters.description: The content type for this file.

functions.name: CURL.GetActiveSocket
functions.description: Receive the active socket used by this curl session. If the socket is no longer valid, -1 is returned. When you finish working with the socket, the CURL.Release closes the socket and cleanup other resources associated with the handle. This is typically used in combination with CURL.SetOptionConnectOnly . This option was added as a replacement for CURL.GetLastSocket since that one isn't working on all platforms.

functions.name: CURL.GetAppConnectTime
functions.description: Queries connect time. Get the time, in seconds, it took from the start until the SSL/SSH connect/handshake to the remote host was completed. This time is most often very near to the PRETRANSFER time, except for cases such as HTTP pippelining where the pretransfer time can be delayed due to waits in line for the pipeline and more.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Show time:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: CURL.GetBatchCurrentFileName
functions.description: Queries current file name. For a batch download with wildcard match, you can query current file name to show in progress dialog. Only available while downloading a file.

functions.name: CURL.GetBatchCurrentFilePath
functions.description: Queries current file path. For a batch download with wildcard match, you can query current file path to show in progress dialog. Only available while downloading a file. Returns the native path to the destination file.

functions.name: CURL.GetBatchDestinationPath
functions.description: Queries destination path. Returns the native path to the destination folder.

functions.name: CURL.GetBatchFileNames
functions.description: Queries list of file names downloaded. While or after transfer you get query list of file names here.

functions.name: CURL.GetCAInfo
functions.description: Queries default CAInfo value.

functions.name: CURL.GetCAPath
functions.description: Queries default CAPath value.

functions.name: CURL.GetCertInfo
functions.description: Queries information on the certificate chain. Returns a list of lists with infos.

examples.label: Query certificate information for MBS website:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
; "https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionCertInfo
";
$curl
; 1)]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Text
;
MBS
("CURL.GetCertInfo";
$curl
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; "https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionCertInfo"; $curl; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetCertInfo"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Text"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetConditionUnmet
functions.description: Get the number 1 if the condition provided in the previous request didn't match. see CURL.SetOptionTimeCondition . Alas, if this returns a 1 you know that the reason you didn't get data in return is because it didn't fulfill the condition. You get a zero if the condition instead was met.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetConnectTime
functions.description: Queries connect time. Get the time, in seconds, it took from the start until the connect to the remote host (or proxy) was completed.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Show time:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Connect Time in seconds" ; MBS( "CURL.GetConnectTime"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$curl ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetContentLengthDownload
functions.description: Get the content-length of the download. This is the value read from the Content-Length: field. Returns -1 if the size isn't known.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetContentLengthUpload
functions.description: Get the specified size of the upload. Returns -1 if the size isn't known.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetContentType
functions.description: Get the content-type of the downloaded object. This is the value read from the Content-Type: field. If you get an empty string, it means that the server didn't send a valid Content-Type header or that the protocol used doesn't support this.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetCookieList
functions.description: Get list of all cookies cURL known (expired ones, too). If there are no cookies (cookies for the handle have not been enabled or simply none have been received), the list is just an empty string.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Perform SOAP service with login and use cookies in second request:
examples.text_code:
# The URL of the service
Set Variable [
$url
; Value: "http://somedomain.com/PartnerService17.asmx" ]
# Build XML for SOAP Login request, e.g. with
MBS
XML
functions or simply subsitute()
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value: "<?xml version=\"
1.0
\" encoding=\"utf-8\"?> <soap:Envelope xmlns:xsi=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance\" xmlns:xsd=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema\" xmlns:soap=\"http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/\">   <soap:Body>       </soap:Body> …" ]
Set Field [
Curl_Test::XML
;
$xml
]
# The SOAP action to use
Set Variable [
$action
; Value: "http://somedomain.com/partner/loginWithLicenceUnlockInfo" ]
#
# Setup transfer
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
$url
) ]
# Put SOAP action and XML data type in the headers
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionHTTPHeader
";
$curl
; "Content-Type: text/xml; charset=utf-8"; "Expect:"; "SOAPAction: \""&
$action
& "\"") ]
# Ask CURL to manage cookies
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionCookieFile
";
$curl
; "" ) ]
# Set the payload for the login transfer
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionPostFields
";
$curl
;
$xml
) ]
# Run transfer
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
# Let's take a look on the cookie list
Set Variable [
$$cookie
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.GetCookieList";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
Curl_Test::Cookie
;
$$cookie
]
# Check result
Set Field [
Curl_Test::Debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
Curl_Test::Resultat
;
$result
]
#
# Reuse same
$curl
for second request reusing existing cookies
#
# Make new XML for second request
Set Variable [
$npk
; Value: "103" ]
# Build XML for SOAP Login request, e.g. with
MBS
XML
functions or simply subsitute()
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value: "<soap:Envelope xmlns:xsi=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance\" xmlns:xsd=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema\" xmlns:soap=\"http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/\">   <soap:Body>       </soap:Body> </soap:Envelope>" ]
Set Field [
Curl_Test::XML
;
$xml
]
Set Variable [
$action
; Value: "http://somedomain.com/partner/queryInfo" ]
#
# Setup transfer
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
$url
) ]
# Put SOAP action and XML data type in the headers
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionHTTPHeader
";
$curl
; "Content-Type: text/xml; charset=utf-8"; "Expect:"; "SOAPAction: \""&
$action
& "\"") ]
# Set the payload for the second transfer
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionPostFields
";
$curl
;
$xml
) ]
# Run transfer
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
# Check result
Set Field [
Curl_Test::Debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
Curl_Test::Resultat
;
$result
]
# Finally end curl session

$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl

functions.name: CURL.GetDebugLength
functions.description: Queries the current length in bytes of the debug messages buffer.

examples.label: Query length. Shows 11 for "Hello World" above:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugLength"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetDebugMessages
functions.description: Returns the debug output of the transaction as text. Use CURL.SetOptionVerbose to enable debug messages. Plugin version 5.2 and newer replace newlines automatically for you to match what FileMaker uses (Mac line endings). See CURL.SetDebugWithData , CURL.SetDebugWithProgress and CURL.SetDebugWithTime to include data/time in the debug log. To view all options you set, use CURL.Options function.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order.

parameters.name: preserveLineEndings
parameters.description: Whether to change line endings to CR for FileMaker. By default (0) we change the line endings to CR, so FileMaker has no trouble. Pass 1 to keep whatever line endings are there.

functions.result: The messages as text or error.

examples.label: Get debug messages:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetDebugWithData
functions.description: Whether to include data in the debug log. By default we don't include all the data in the debug log and just the messages. Returns 1 if data is included or 0 if not.

functions.name: CURL.GetDebugWithProgress
functions.description: Queries whether to include progress in debug messages. Returns 1 if enabled or 0 if disabled.

functions.name: CURL.GetDebugWithTime
functions.description: Queries whether times are included in debug messages. By default this is off.

functions.name: CURL.GetEarlyDataSent
functions.description: Get the number of bytes sent as TLS early data. TLS early data is only attempted when kSSLOptionEarlyData is set for the transfer. In addition, it is only used by libcurl when a TLS session exists that announces support. The amount is negative when the sent data was rejected by the server. TLS allows a server that announces support for early data to reject any attempt to use it at its own discretion. When for example 127 bytes had been sent, but were rejected, it reports -127 as the amount "sent".

functions.name: CURL.GetEffectiveMethod
functions.description: Get the last used effective HTTP method. In cases when you've asked libcurl to follow redirects, the method may very well not be the same method the first request would use.

examples.label: Query it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "EffectiveMethod" ; MBS( "CURL.GetEffectiveMethod"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$curl ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetEffectiveURL
functions.description: Get the last used effective URL.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Follow redirection and get effective URL:
examples.text_code:
#Start new session
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
#Set URL to load (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, etc.)
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
CURL Test::URL
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionFollowLocation
";
$curl
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:

("
CURL.SetOptionMaxRedirs
";
$curl
; 3)]
#RUN now
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
#Check result
Set Field [
CURL Test::Text
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
; "UTF8")]
Set Field [
CURL Test::newURL
;
MBS
("CURL.GetEffectiveURL";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
CURL Test::debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
#Cleanup

$result
; Value:

";
$curl
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Start new session</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set URL to load (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, etc.)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; CURL Test::URL)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionFollowLocation"; $curl; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionMaxRedirs"; $curl; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>RUN now</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResultAsText"; $curl; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Text"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetEffectiveURL"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="newURL"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="debug"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetFTPEntryPath
functions.description: Get a string holding the path of the entry path. That is the initial path libcurl ended up in when logging on to the remote FTP server. This stores a NULL as pointer if something is wrong.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetFailedEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to be run when transfer failed.

functions.name: CURL.GetFailedFileName
functions.description: Queries the file name for the script to call in case of failed transfer.

functions.name: CURL.GetFailedScriptName
functions.description: Queries the script name for the script to call in case of failed transfer.

functions.name: CURL.GetFileTime
functions.description: Get the remote time of the retrieved document. If you get an empty result, it can be because of many reasons (unknown, the server hides it or the server doesn't support the command that tells document time etc) and the time of the document is unknown. Note that you must tell the server to collect this information before the transfer is made, by using the CURL.SetOptionFileTime or you will unconditionally get an empty result back. e.g. "28.02.2013 16:43:55"
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Try getting the file time:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
# put in some URL
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
; "https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/examples/test.txt") ]
# Only check if file exists, but don't download it
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionNoBody
";
$curl
; 1) ]
# Request to read file time
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionFileTime
";
$curl
; 1) ]
# perform request
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
#
# check result
Set Field [
CURL Test::Debug
Messages ;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
# check response code: 200 if file exists or 404 if not found
Set Variable [
$responseCode
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResponseCode
";
$curl
) ]
# and get the time value
Set Variable [
$time
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.GetFileTime";
$curl
) ]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]

functions.name: CURL.GetHTTPAuthAvail
functions.description: Get a bitmask indicating the authentication method(s) available. The meaning of the bits is explained in CURL.SetOptionHTTPAuth .
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetHTTPConnectCode
functions.description: Get the last received proxy response code to a CONNECT request.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetHTTPVersion
functions.description: Queries http version. This function checks for a license. Created 15th April 2018 , last changed 16th April 2018 CURL.GetHTTPConnectCode - CURL.GetHeaderAsJSON 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: CURL.GetHeaderAsJSON
functions.description: Returns headers parsed as JSON. This is for HTTP headers or headers in similar format. We add to the JSON some special entries for the HTTP result line: HTTP-Version, HTTP-ResponseCode, and HTTP-ResponseMessage.

examples.label: Query headers as JSON:
examples.text_code:

( "CURL.GetHeaderAsJSON";
$curl
)

:
"1.1"
,

:
"200"
,
"HTTP-ResponseMessage"
:
"OK"
,

:
"Sat, 25 Apr 2020 10:37:35 GMT"
,
"Server"
:
"Apache/2.4.41"
,
"Upgrade"
:
"h2c"
,
"Connection"
:
"Upgrade"
,
"Last-Modified"
:
"Thu, 28 Feb 2013 16:43:55 GMT"
,
"ETag"
:
"\"
726-4d6cb98aca8c0\
""
,
"Accept-Ranges"
:
"bytes"
,
"Content-Length"
:
"1830"
,
"Content-Type"
:
"text/plain"
,
"Content-Language"
:
"en"
}

functions.name: CURL.GetHeaderSize
functions.description: Get the total size of all the headers received. Measured in number of bytes.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetHeaders
functions.description: Returns the headers of the transaction as text. You are responsible for detecting decoding. Plugin version 5.2 and newer replace newlines automatically for you to match what FileMaker uses (Mac line endings).

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order.

parameters.name: preserveLineEndings
parameters.description: Whether to change line endings to CR for FileMaker. By default (0) we change the line endings to CR, so FileMaker has no trouble. Pass 1 to keep whatever line endings are there.

functions.result: The headers as text or error.

examples.label: Get header lines:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetHeaders”; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Get file name for container from header:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$name
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$headers
; Value:
MBS
( "CURL.GetHeaders”;
$curl
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$headers
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.MatchesPrefix
";
$headers
; "Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=") ]
If [ Length(
$headers
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value: Middle (
$headers
; 43 ; Length(
$headers
) ) ]
End If
Set Field [
CURL Test::File
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsContainer
";
$curl
;
$name
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.GetHeaders”; $curl; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$headers</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "List.MatchesPrefix"; $headers; "Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$headers</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length($headers) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Middle ( $headers ; 43 ; Length($headers) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResultAsContainer"; $curl; $name) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="File "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetInputAsText
functions.description: Returns the input of the transaction as text. You are responsible for detecting decoding. Plugin version 5.2 and newer replace newlines automatically for you to match what FileMaker uses (Mac line endings).

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

parameters.name: preserveLineEndings
parameters.description: Whether to change line endings to CR for FileMaker. By default (0) we change the line endings to CR, so FileMaker has no trouble. Pass 1 to keep whatever line endings are there.

examples.label: Write input to a text file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
";
MBS
( "CURL.GetInputAsText";
$curl
; "UTF-8"; 1 ); "/tmp/test.txt" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; MBS( "CURL.GetInputAsText"; $curl; "UTF-8"; 1 ); "/tmp/test.txt" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetLocalIP
functions.description: Queries local IP. Get a string holding the local (source) IP address of the most recent connection done with this curl handle. This string may be IPv6 if that's enabled.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetLocalPort
functions.description: Get the local (source) port of the most recent connection done with this curl handle.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetMQTTFileName
functions.description: Queries script name set to run for incoming MQTT file name.

functions.name: CURL.GetMQTTMessages
functions.description: Queries JSON array with messages. Each JSON object in the array has content and topic properties.

functions.name: CURL.GetMQTTScriptName
functions.description: Queries script name set to run for incoming MQTT script name.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionChunkLengthPenaltySize
functions.description: Queries chunk length penalty size. A connection with a chunk length longer than this will not be considered for pipelining.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionContentLengthPenaltySize
functions.description: Queries content length penalty size. A connection with a content-length longer than this will not be considered for pipelining.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionMaxConcurrentStreams
functions.description: Queries max connections per server.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionMaxConnects
functions.description: Queries max connects setting. Maximum number of entries in the connection cache.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionMaxHostConnections
functions.description: Queries max host connections value. Maximum number of (pipelining) connections to one host.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionMaxPipelineLength
functions.description: Queries max pipeline length. Maximum number of requests in a pipeline.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionMaxTotalConnections
functions.description: Queries max total connections value. Maximum number of open connections in total.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiOptionPipelining
functions.description: Queries pipelining mode. Is 1 to enable pipelining for this multi handle.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiRunningTransfers
functions.description: Queries running transfers count. Only for asynchronous transfers with CURL.PerformAsync . Value is -1 till initialized.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiTransfersAdded
functions.description: The cumulative number of all easy handles added to the multi, ever. This includes internal handles added for tasks (like resolving via DoH, for example). For the current number of easy handles managed by the multi, use TransfersCurrent.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiTransfersCurrent
functions.description: Returns the number of easy handles currently added to the multi handle. This does not include already removed handles. It does include internal handles that get added for tasks (like resolving via DoH, for example). For the total number of easy handles ever added to the multi, see CURL.GetMultiTransfersAdded .

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiTransfersDone
functions.description: The number of easy handles currently finished, but not yet removed.

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiTransfersPending
functions.description: The number of current easy handles waiting to start. An added transfer might become pending for various reasons: a connection limit forces it to wait, resolving DNS is not finished or it is not clear if an existing, matching connection may allow multiplexing (HTTP/2 or HTTP/3).

functions.name: CURL.GetMultiTransfersRunning
functions.description: The number of easy handles currently running, e.g. where the transfer has started but not finished yet.

functions.name: CURL.GetNameLookupTime
functions.description: Get the time, in seconds, it took from the start until the name resolving was completed.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Show time:

functions.name: CURL.GetNumConnects
functions.description: Queries new connections count for transfer. Query how many new connections libcurl had to create to achieve the previous transfer (only the successful connects are counted). Combined with CURL.SetOptionRedirectCount you are able to know how many times libcurl successfully reused existing connection(s) or not.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetOSErrNo
functions.description: Get the errno variable from a connect failure. Note that the value is only set on failure, it is not reset upon a successfull operation.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetProxySSLVerifyResult
functions.description: Queries result of proxy ssl verification.

functions.name: CURL.GetRTSPCSEQRecv
functions.description: Query RTSP sequence counter received. This function checks for a license. Created 12nd July 2015 , last changed 12nd July 2015 CURL.GetProxySSLVerifyResult - CURL.GetRTSPClientCSEQ 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.description: Query RTSP Client sequence counter.

functions.name: CURL.GetRedirectCount
functions.description: Get the total number of redirections that were actually followed.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetRedirectTime
functions.description: Queries redirect time. Get the total time, in seconds, it took for all redirection steps include name lookup, connect, pretransfer and transfer before final transaction was started. CURL.GetRedirectTime contains the complete execution time for multiple redirections.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetRedirectURL
functions.description: Queries redirect URL. Get the URL a redirect would take you to if you would enable CURL.SetOptionFollowLocation . This can come very handy if you think using the built-in libcurl redirect logic isn't good enough for you but you would still prefer to avoid implementing all the magic of figuring out the new URL.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: Do not follow, but return redirect URL:
examples.text_code:
# Start new session
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
# Set URL to load
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
;
CURL Test::URL
) ]
# RUN now
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
# Check result
Set Field [
CURL Test::Text
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
; "UTF8") ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::newURL
;
MBS
("CURL.GetRedirectURL";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]

functions.name: CURL.GetReferer
functions.description: Queries referrer text.

functions.name: CURL.GetRequestSize
functions.description: Get the total size of the issued requests. This is so far only for HTTP requests. Note that this may be more than one request if CURL.SetOptionFollowLocation is true.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

functions.name: CURL.GetResponseCode
functions.description: Get the last received HTTP or FTP code. This will be zero if no server response code has been received. Note that a proxy's CONNECT response should be read with CURL.GetHTTPConnectCode and not this. For HTTP this is the normal status code. e.g. 200 for OK and 404 for file not found.
functions.result: Returns the requested value or nothing on error.

examples.label: On success puts download in container, else shows failed:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$errorCode
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.ErrorCode
";
$curl
)]
Set Variable [
$httpResult
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.GetResponseCode";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
Download Async::Result
;
$errorCode
]
Set Field [
Download Async::DebugMessages
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
If [
$errorCode
= 0 and
$httpResult
= 200]
Set Field [
Download Async::Progress
; "Done"]
Set Field [
Download Async::Output
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsContainer
";
$curl
)]
Else
Set Field [
Download Async::Progress
; "Failed"]
Set Field [
Download Async::Output
; ""]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.ErrorCode"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorCode</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResponseCode"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$httpResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$errorCode</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="DebugMessages"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$errorCode = 0 and $httpResult = 200</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Done"</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Progress"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResultAsContainer"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Output"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Failed"</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Progress"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    <Field table="Download Async" id="99999" name="Output"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.GetResultAsContainer
functions.description: Returns the result of the transaction as container value with auto detection of type. The plugin will detect the type of data based on the file extension. If no filename is provided, the plugin uses the filename from the URL. For PDF content: If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit. For HEIF/HEIC images, please use Container.ReadImage to convert the image.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The desired file name. Since 13.3 only used if download has no file name.

parameters.name: PluginDetect
parameters.description: If 1 the plugin will do the file type detection. If not provided or 0 and the FileMaker version is 13.0 or newer, the FileMaker engine will decide on the type.

examples.label: Load JPEG from URL to container:
examples.text_code:
# start new transfer
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
# set URL
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
; "https://monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/images/devcon2013.jpg")]
# run transfer
Set Variable [
$ErrorCode
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
# get result as JPG and store in field:
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
( "CURL.GetResultAsContainer";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
test::MyContainerField
;
$Result
]
# get debug messages:
Set Variable [
$DebugMessages
; Value:
MBS
( "
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
)]

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
