functions.name: CURL.SetOptionUserName
functions.description:
Sets the user name string for the transfer. In order to specify the password to be used in conjunction with the user name use the CURL.SetOptionPassword option. Please check case as some services check names case sensitive. See also USERNAME option in CURL manual. Examples Set login credentials: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl ; Logins::Username ) ] Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionPassword "; $curl ; Logins::Password ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl; Logins::Username) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionPassword"; $curl; Logins::Password) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Setup for IMAP with Google GMail: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $curl ; Value: MBS (" CURL.New ") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionPort "; $curl ; 993) // IMAP SSL port ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionUseSSL "; $curl ; 3) // require SSL ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionSSLVersion "; $curl ; 6) // TLSv1 .2 ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionURL "; $curl ; "imaps://imap.gmail.com/INBOX") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl ; $user ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionPassword "; $curl ; $pass ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionSSLVersion"; $curl; 6) // TLSv1.2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; "imaps://imap.gmail.com/INBOX") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl; $user) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Setup IMAP for Office 365: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionURL "; $curl ; "imaps://outlook.office365.com/INBOX") ] # your oAuth token Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionXOAuth2Bearer "; $curl ; "eyJ0eXAiOi...") ] # no password needed! Just username: Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl ; "test@outlook.com") ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: CURL.SetOptionVerbose
functions.description:
Whether to store log messages. Set the parameter to 1 to get curl to display a lot of verbose information about its operations. Very useful protocol debugging and understanding. You can get the debug messages with CURL.GetDebugMessages . Since version 5.2 of our plugin, this is on by default. See CURL.SetDebugWithData , CURL.SetDebugWithProgress and CURL.SetDebugWithTime to include data/time in the debug log. See also VERBOSE option in CURL manual. Examples Set verbose mode: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionVerbose"; $curl ; 1) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionVerbose"; $curl; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: CURL.SetOptionWebSocketOptions
functions.description: Sets WebSocket behaviour options. Pass a bit mask to tell CURL about specific WebSocket behaviors. To detach a WebSocket connection and use the CURL.WebSocketSend and CURL.WebSocketReceive functions after the HTTP upgrade procedure, set the CURL.SetOptionConnectOnly option to 2. Available bits in the bitmask: 1: Deliver "raw" WebSocket traffic to the Write event. 2: No auto pong. In raw mode, CURL does not handle pings or any other frame for the application.

functions.name: CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch
functions.description:
Enable wildcard matching. Set parameter to 1 if you want to transfer multiple files according to a file name pattern. The pattern can be specified as part of the OptionURL option, using an fnmatch-like pattern (Shell Pattern Matching) in the last part of URL (file name). By default, libcurl uses its internal wildcard matching implementation. A brief introduction of its syntax follows: - ASTERISK ftp://example.com/some/path/*.txt (for all txt's from the root directory) ? - QUESTION MARK Question mark matches any (exactly one) character. ftp://example.com/some/path/photo?.jpeg [ - BRACKET EXPRESSION The left bracket opens a bracket expression. The question mark and asterisk have no special meaning in a bracket expression. Each bracket expression ends by the right bracket and matches exactly one character. Some examples follow: [a-zA-Z0-9] or [f-gF-G] - character interval [abc] - character enumeration [^abc] or [!abc] - negation [[:name:]] class expression. Supported classes are alnum,lower, space, alpha, digit, print, upper, blank, graph, xdigit. [][-!^] - special case - matches only '-', ']', '[', '!' or '^'. These characters have no special purpose. [\[\]\\] - escape syntax. Matches '[', ']' or '´. Using the rules above, a file name pattern can be constructed: ftp://example.com/some/path/[a-z[:upper:]\\].jpeg This feature is only supported for FTP download. See also WILDCARDMATCH option in CURL manual. Examples Enable wildcard match: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( "CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch"; $curl ; 1 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch"; $curl; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Download several files via FTP: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $curl ; Value: MBS (" CURL.New ")] #Set URL with wildcard match Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionURL "; $curl ; CURL Test::URL )] #Set folder where to put files Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetBatchDestinationPath "; $curl ; CURL Test::DestPath )] #credentials... Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionPassword "; $curl ; CURL Test::Password )] Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionUserName "; $curl ; CURL Test::Name )] #enable wildcard match Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch"; $curl ; 1)] #download them all Set Field [ CURL Test::Result ; MBS (" CURL.Perform "; $curl )] #check results Set Field [ CURL Test::debug ; MBS (" CURL.GetDebugMessages "; $curl )] Set Field [ CURL Test::FileNames ; MBS (" CURL.GetBatchFileNames "; $curl )] Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS (" CURL.Release "; $curl )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch"; $curl; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>download them all</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>check results</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="debug"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetBatchFileNames"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="FileNames"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.SetOptionXOAuth2Bearer
functions.description:
The XOAUTH2 bearer token. For open authentication used with web services. No Bearer prefix please. If used, please don't call CURL.SetOptionUserName and CURL.SetOptionPassword as that would overwrite the token. Use e.g. with google mail. See also XOAUTH2_BEARER option in CURL manual. Examples Setup oAuth for SMTP with user name, bearer and no password: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" SendMail.SetSMTPUserName "; $EmailID ; "test@gmail.com") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" SendMail.SetSMTPPassword "; $EmailID ; "" // empty ) ] # ... Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionXOAuth2Bearer"; $curl ; "dXNlcj1zb21...") ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.SetSMTPUsername"; $EmailID; "test@gmail.com") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.SetSMTPPassword"; $EmailID; "" // empty ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionXOAuth2Bearer"; $curl; "dXNlcj1zb21...") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Setup IMAP for Office 365: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionURL "; $curl ; "imaps://outlook.office365.com/INBOX") ] # your oAuth token Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("CURL.SetOptionXOAuth2Bearer"; $curl ; "eyJ0eXAiOi...") ] # no password needed! Just username: Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" CURL.SetOptionUserName "; $curl ; "test@outlook.com") ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; "imaps://outlook.office365.com/INBOX") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your oAuth token</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionXOAuth2Bearer"; $curl; "eyJ0eXAiOi...") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> no password needed! Just username:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUserName"; $curl; "test@outlook.com") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also CURL.SetOptionAutoReferer CURL.SetOptionPassword CURL.SetOptionUploadFlags CURL.SetOptionURL CURL.SetOptionUserName SendMail.SetSMTPPassword SendMail.SetSMTPUserName Example Databases CURL/Email/GMail oAuth SMTP CURL/Email/IMAP Email List CURL/Email/IMAP Email Upload CURL/Email/IMAP Email CURL/Email/List IMAP folders CURL/Email/Office 365 oAuth SMTP CURL/WebServices/ebay webservice/ebay Webservice Blog Entries IMAP Upload Flags Using Gmail with oAuth2 for SMTP with MBS Plugin More follow up on oAuth for Office 365 SMTP with OAuth for Office 365 in FileMaker GMail for emails with MBS Plugin Created 15th April 2018 , last changed 7th September 2025 CURL.SetOptionWildCardMatch - CURL.SetProgressEvaluate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: CURL.SetProgressEvaluate
functions.description: Sets expression to be run when transfer makes progress. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the curl reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: CURL.SetProgressScript
functions.description: Sets the progress script. This script is called for asynchronous transfers when percent value changed. So you can update progress user interface. Does not work on server due to missing capability to trigger scripts in the plugin SDK with FileMaker Server. We trigger script/evaluate only if percent value changes. And we defer next invocation until you call CURL.GetProgressCurrentDownload , CURL.GetProgressCurrentUpload , CURL.GetProgressPercent , CURL.GetProgressTotalDownload , and CURL.GetProgressTotalUpload so script triggers don't queue up. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

examples.label: Start a curl session and setup a few scripts:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetFinishedScript
";
$curl
; Get(FileName); "DownloadFinished")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.SetProgressScript";
$curl
; Get(FileName); "ProgressScript")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetFinishedScript"; $curl; Get(FileName); "DownloadFinished")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetProgressScript"; $curl; Get(FileName); "ProgressScript")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.SetSucceededEvaluate
functions.description: Sets expression to be run when transfer succeeded. Works with CURL.Perform , CURL.PerformAsync and CURL.PerformInBackground . In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the curl reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed. The transfer is successful if CURL.Perform internally returns no error. For http requests we also check response code for an error.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

examples.label: Set what to do when transfer succeeds:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.SetSucceededEvaluate";
$curl
; "
MBS
(\"
CURL.Release
\";
$$ID
$$)" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetSucceededEvaluate"; $curl; "MBS(\"CURL.Release\"; $$ID$$)" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.SetSucceededScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call when a transfer succeeded. Works with CURL.Perform , CURL.PerformAsync and CURL.PerformInBackground . Does not work on server due to missing capability to trigger scripts in the plugin SDK with FileMaker Server. The transfer is successful if CURL.Perform internally returns no error. For http requests we also check response code for an error.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

examples.label: Set scripts to run on success or failure:
examples.text_code:

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.SetSucceededScript";
$curl
; Get(FileName); "Transfer Success" ) ]
# in case of failure, trigger script to log error
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetFailedScript
";
$curl
; Get(FileName); "Transfer Failed" ) ]

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: CURL.SetupAWS
functions.signature: MBS( "CURL.SetupAWS"; curl; AWSAccessKeyId; AWSSecretAccessKey; Region; Service; Path; Domain; Verb { ; HashedPayload; Headers; QueryParameters } )
functions.description: Setup CURL to transfer to Amazon Webservices. This implements AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 signature for credentials, sets authentication, URL, HTTP Headers and other parameters. For upload or post, please set input parameters first. When using CURL.OpenInputFile to stream data, you need to calculate the payload hash yourself (with Hash.Digest ) and pass it here as parameter. Works also for other compatible S3 services beside Amazon's like the one from Wasabi or Dell ECS. Please be aware that this function depends on a correct time and date set on the computer. If the time is not set correct, the authentication will fail. See also CURL.SetOptionAWSSigV4 function to enable CURL’s built-in AWS signature calculation.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path for the URL. Should always start with "/".

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: Optional the domain to use. By default we just build it from region and service.

parameters.name: Verb
parameters.description: The HTTP Operation to do. Can be POST, PUT, GET, PATCH, HEAD or DELETE. Added COPY and MOVE for v15.4.

parameters.name: HashedPayload
parameters.description: The hashed payload. If empty, we calculate it automatically from input data or postfields. This is a lowercase hex SHA256.

parameters.name: Headers
parameters.description: Extra HTTP headers to include. Here you can specify various headers to include in signature and request.

parameters.name: QueryParameters
parameters.description: List of query parameters to include. Keys and values must be URL encoded using Text.EncodeURLComponent . Keys may need to be lowercase.

examples.label: Upload file from disk and calculate hash ourselves:
examples.text_code:
# Build a path to file (local) and in AWS
Set Variable [
$Filename
; Value: "test.pdf" ]
Set Variable [
$Path
; Value: "/" &
$BucketName
& "/PDF/" &
$Filename
]
Set Variable [
$localPath
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/license.pdf" ]
# calculate hash for file to upload
Set Variable [
$HashedPayload
; Value: Lower(
MBS
( "
Hash.Digest
"; "sha256"; "path";
$localPath
; ""; "hex" )) ]
# upload
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
# file content is streamed
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.OpenInputFile
";
$curl
;
$localPath
) ]
# setup AWS
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.SetupAWS";
$curl
;
$AWSAccessKeyId
;
$AWSSecretAccessKey
;
$Region
;
$Service
;
$Path
;
$Domain
;
$Verb
;
$HashedPayload
;
$headers
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
Set Field [
CURL Test::debug
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Build a path to file (local) and in AWS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"test.pdf" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/" & $BucketName & "/PDF/" & $Filename </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/license.pdf" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$localPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate hash for file to upload</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Lower(MBS( "Hash.Digest"; "sha256"; "path"; $localPath; ""; "hex" )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$HashedPayload</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> upload</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> file content is streamed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.OpenInputFile"; $curl; $localPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> setup AWS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetupAWS"; $curl; $AWSAccessKeyId; $AWSSecretAccessKey; $Region; $Service; $Path; $Domain; $Verb; $HashedPayload; $headers) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl) </Calculation>
    <Field table="CURL Test" id="99999" name="debug "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CURL.SetupOAuth
functions.signature: MBS( "CURL.SetupOAuth"; curl; ConsumerKey; ConsumerSecret; AccessToken; AccessTokenSecret; HTTPVerb; URL { ; Parameters; Headers; Datas; Nonce; TimeStamp; HMAC; Verifier; Realm } )
functions.description: Setups an OAuth signed transfer. The plugin calculates the signature and adds headers for authorization to the CURL handle. Sets URL with parameters and optionally data values for POST.

parameters.name: AccessToken
parameters.description: The oauth access token. Can be empty if not yet known.

parameters.name: AccessTokenSecret
parameters.description: The oauth access token secret. Can be empty if not yet known.

parameters.name: HTTPVerb
parameters.description: The HTTP verb. Can be POST, PUT, GET, DELETE or HEAD. Default if empty is GET.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL to query without parameters.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.description: List of parameters. Can be empty. Please pass list with newline as delimiter. values should be URL encoded already.

parameters.name: Headers
parameters.description: List of additional header entries to include. Can be empty. We automatically set Authorization header.

parameters.name: Datas
parameters.description: Data value list for POST. Can be old stype key=value for form data. Or can be JSON, XML or whatever else the API expects as payload.

parameters.name: Nonce
parameters.description: The nonce value. If empty, we generate it automatically for you.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp to use for signature. If empty, we query current timestamp (same as you calling Time.UnixTimeStamp )

parameters.name: HMAC
parameters.description: Which HMAC algorithm to use. Can be HMAC-SHA512, HMAC-SHA256 or HMAC-SHA1. Default is HMAC-SHA1 if parameter is missing or empty.

parameters.name: Verifier
parameters.description: The verifier. Usually an ID shown on a website after login to the service and authorization of an app.

parameters.name: Realm
parameters.description: The realm value, e.g. for account name. Pass empty if not used.

examples.label: Setup OAuth query for Twitter:
examples.text_code:
# global settings (Sample entries from Twitter documentation)
# https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/basics/authentication/guides/creating-a-signature.html
#
Set Variable [
$ConsumerKey
; Value: "xvz1evFS4wEEPTGEFPHBog" ]
Set Variable [
$ConsumerSecret
; Value: "kAcSOqF21Fu85e7zjz7ZN2U4ZRhfV3WpwPAoE3Z7kBw" ]
Set Variable [
$AccessToken
; Value: "370773112-GmHxMAgYyLbNEtIKZeRNFsMKPR9EyMZeS9weJAEb" ]
Set Variable [
$AccessTokenSecret
; Value: "LswwdoUaIvS8ltyTt5jkRh4J50vUPVVHtR2YPi5kE" ]
Set Variable [
$nonce
; Value: "kYjzVBB8Y0ZFabxSWbWovY3uYSQ2pTgmZeNu2VS4cg" ]
Set Variable [
$TimeStamp
; Value: "1318622958" ]
#
Set Variable [
$URL
; Value: "https://api.twitter.com/
1.1
/statuses/update.json" ]
Set Variable [
$Method
; Value: "POST" ]
#
Set Variable [
$Parameters
; Value: "include_entities=true" ]
Set Variable [
$Headers
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$Data
; Value: "status=Hello%20Ladies%20%2B%20Gentlemen%2C%20a%20signed%20OAuth%20request%21" ]
#
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("CURL.SetupOAuth";
$curl
;
$ConsumerKey
;
$ConsumerSecret
;
$AccessToken
;
$AccessTokenSecret
;
$Method
;
$URL
;
$Parameters
;
$Headers
;
$Data
;
$Nonce
;
$Timestamp
) ]
# Perform...
#
# signature must be hCtSmYh+iHYCEqBWrE7C7hYmtUk=
#
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]

; Value: "http://xxxx/index.php/rest/V1/customers/" &
$customerID
]
Set Variable [
$Method
; Value: "GET" ]
# Optional parameters for filters
Set Variable [
$Parameters
; Value: "abc=hello¶test=world" ]
#
# Start new session
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:

("
CURL.New
") ]

$curl
;
$ConsumerKey
;
$ConsumerSecret
;
$AccessToken
;
$AccessTokenSecret
;
$Method
;
$URL
;
$Parameters
; "") ]
# RUN now
Set Field [
CURL::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
) ]
# Check result
Set Field [
CURL::HTTP
Response ;
MBS

";
$curl

";

";
$curl
) ]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:

("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]

functions.name: CURL.Upkeep
functions.description: Perform any connection upkeep checks. Some protocols have "connection upkeep" mechanisms. These mechanisms usually send some traffic on existing connections in order to keep them alive; this can prevent connections from being closed due to overzealous firewalls, for example. Currently the only protocol with a connection upkeep mechanism is HTTP/2: when the connection upkeep interval is exceeded and CURL.Upkeep is called, an HTTP/2 PING frame is sent on the connection. This function must be explicitly called in order to perform the upkeep work. The connection upkeep interval is set with CURL.SetOptionUpkeepIntervalMS .

functions.name: CURL.UseSystemCertificates
functions.description: Read certificate from system and installs them in CURL session,. For macOS and Windows we load the available certificates from keychain or Windows Certificate Store and pass them to CURL. This is better than having no certificates at all. Even better is normally to provide a cacert.pem file with only the expected certificates. Returns number of certificates loaded. No longer needed since MBS Plugin v16.0 with CURL 8.18 since CURL will query certificate storage in keychain automatically.

functions.name: CURL.WebSocketReceive
functions.description: Receive WebSocket data. Retrieves as much as possible of a received WebSocket data fragment into the buffer, but not more than BufferSize bytes. The bytesReceived value in the returned JSON is set to the number of bytes actually stored. If there is more fragment data to deliver than what fits in the provided buffer, CURL returns a full buffer and the application needs to call this function again to continue draining the buffer. The returned JSON contains the data and the metadata about the received data. This includes the following fields:

parameters.name: BufferSize
parameters.description: The buffer size to use. Default is 65536 and minimum is 1024.

functions.name: CURL.WebSocketSend
functions.signature: MBS( "CURL.WebSocketSend"; curl; Text { ; Encoding; FrameSize; SendFlags } )
functions.description: Send the specific message fragment over an established WebSocket connection. The text parameter holds the data to send. Returns the number of payload bytes actually sent. To send a (huge) fragment using multiple calls with partial content per invoke, set the offset flag (32) in the flags and the FrameSize argument as the total expected size for the first part, then set the offset flag (32) with a zero FrameSize for the following parts. If not sending a partial fragment or if this is raw mode, FrameSize should be set to zero. If kWebSocketRaw is enabled in WebSocketOptions, the flags argument should be set to 0.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8.

parameters.name: FrameSize
parameters.description: The frame size. Default is zero.

parameters.name: SendFlags
parameters.description: The send flags. Default is zero.

; Value:

("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
) ]
